module: Module 7 - AWS Databases and Database Services

sections.title: Core Idea
points: Databases store, organize, retrieve, and manage data.
AWS offers purpose-built databases for different workloads.
Choose the database based on access pattern, scale, structure, and performance needs.
sections.exam_tip: AWS exams often test matching workload to the correct database type.

sections.title: Main Database Categories
sections.exam_tip: Know the category before memorizing service names.

sections.title: Amazon RDS
points: Supports MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server.
AWS handles patching, backups, hardware provisioning.
Good for traditional applications needing SQL.
sections.exam_tip: Need managed SQL database = Amazon RDS.

sections.title: Amazon Aurora
points: High-performance relational database compatible with MySQL and PostgreSQL.
More scalable and faster than standard MySQL in many workloads.

sections.title: Amazon DynamoDB
sections.exam_tip: Need serverless NoSQL at scale = DynamoDB.

sections.title: Amazon ElastiCache
points: Used to reduce database load and improve speed.
Great for sessions, leaderboards, hot data.
sections.exam_tip: Need faster reads / cache layer = ElastiCache.

sections.title: Amazon Neptune
points: Examples: fraud detection, recommendation engines, social networks.

sections.title: Amazon Redshift
points: Used for analytics on huge datasets.
Supports SQL queries and BI dashboards.

sections.title: AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS)
points: Can keep source database running during migration.

sections.title: AWS Backup
points: Centralized backup management across AWS services.
sections.exam_tip: Need one backup solution across services = AWS Backup.

sections.title: High Availability Terms
sections.exam_tip: Failover = Multi-AZ | Read scaling = Read Replica.

sections.title: Managed Service Advantage
points: Customer manages schema, users, permissions, data.
sections.exam_tip: AWS = of the cloud | Customer = in the cloud.

sections.title: Common Exam Traps
points: ElastiCache is cache, not primary database.
RDS is transactional DB, not analytics warehouse.
Neptune is graph, not document DB.
Redshift is analytics, not OLTP transactional system.
sections.exam_tip: AWS loves asking what service does NOT fit.
